metadata.title: Gender-Related Differences in the Metabolic Response to Fasting
metadata.journal: The Journal of Clinical Endocrinology & Metabolism
metadata.study_design: Experimental study with stable isotope technique and hyperinsulinemic euglycemic clamp
metadata.demographics: Ten male and ten female subjects, age 18-35 years, Caucasian race, BMI 20-25 kg/m², no excessive sport activities, no medication, women studied during follicular phase of menstrual cycle

evidence.quote: Gender-Related Differences in the Metabolic Response to Fasting
TheJournalofClinicalEndocrinology&Metabolism92(9):3646–3652
Maarten R. Soeters, Hans P. Sauerwein, Johanna E. Groener, Johannes M. Aerts, Mariëtte T. Ackermans, Jan F. C. Glatz, Eric Fliers, and Mireille J. Serlie
Aftera38-hfast,measurementsofglucoseandlipidfluxesandmuscleceramideandfattyacidtranslocase/CD36wereperformedbeforeandafterahyperinsulinemiceuglycemicclamp.

Tenmaleand10femalesubjectswererecruitedviaadvertisementsinlocalmagazines. Criteriaforinclusionwere 1) absenceofafamilyhistoryofdiabetes; 2) age 18–35 yr; 3) Caucasianrace; 4) bodymassindex(BMI)20–25kg/m2; 5) noexcessivesportactivities,i.e.fewerthanthreetimesperweek;and6) nomedication. Womenwerestudiedduringthefollicularphaseofthemenstrualcycle.
Ten male and 10 female subjects were recruited via advertisements in local magazines.
Criteria for inclusion were 1) absence of a family history of diabetes; 2) age 18–35 yr
4) body mass index (BMI) 20–25 kg/m2

methods_participants.label: Women menstrual cycle phase
methods_participants.method: fasting from 2000h 2d before study start until end of study

evidence.quote: the subjects were fasting from 2000h 2d before the start of the study until the end of the study
[1,1,2,3,3-2H5]glycerol (prime, 1.6 μmol/kg; continuous, 0.11 μmol/kg·min)
infusions of insulin (60mU/m2·min) (Actrapid 100 IU/ml; NovoNordisk FarmaB.V., Alphen aan den Rijn, The Netherlands)
Plasma glucose concentrations were measured with the glucose oxidase method using a Beckman Glucose Analyzer 2 (Beckman, Palo Alto, CA) with intraassay variation of 2–3%.

methods_participants.label: Muscle biopsy timing
methods_participants.method: Pro-Mag I biopsy needle under local anesthesia

evidence.quote: Muscle biopsies were performed to assess muscle content of ceramide, glucosylceramide, and FAT/CD36 at the end of both the basal state and the hyperinsulinemic euglycemic clamp.
Body composition was measured with bioelectrical impedance analysis (Maltron BF906; Maltron, Rayleigh, UK).

methods_participants.label: Blood sampling frequency during equilibration
methods_participants.method: five blood samples drawn at 5-min intervals

evidence.quote: At T = 8h, five blood samples were drawn at 5-min intervals for determination of glucose and glycerol enrichments.

methods_participants.label: Pre-study diet
methods_participants.value: weight-maintaining diet containing at least 250g carbohydrates per day for 3 days

evidence.quote: For 3d before the fasting period, all volunteers consumed a weight-maintaining diet containing at least 250 g carbohydrates per day.

All samples were diluted to the same protein concentration and mixed with sample buffer (4:1, vol/vol) before being subjected to SDS-PAGE and immunoblotting exactly as described before (34). The CD36 antigen-antibody band at 88 kDa was visualized with a chemiluminescence substrate (ECL; Amersham Biosciences UK Ltd., Buckinghamshire, UK)
saline was infused as NaCl 0.9% at a rate of 50 ml/h to keep the catheters patent.

narrative_results.statement: Plasma epinephrine was significantly lower in females than males during the hyperinsulinemic euglycemic clamp

evidence.quote: Plasma epinephrine was significantly lower in females than males during the hyperinsulinemic euglycemic clamp

narrative_results.statement: Insulin-mediated peripheral glucose uptake was not different between sexes

evidence.quote: Insulin-mediated peripheral glucose uptake was not different between sexes
Insulin-mediated peripheral glucose uptake during the clamp (Rd) did not differ significantly between men and women

narrative_results.statement: Muscle fatty acid translocase/CD36 was not different between sexes in the basal state and during the clamp

evidence.quote: Muscle fatty acid translocase/CD36 was not different between sexes in the basal state and during the clamp
There were no differences in muscle FAT/CD36 content between women and men during both the basal state and the hyperinsulinemic euglycemic clamp

narrative_results.statement: Women are relatively protected from FFA-induced insulin resistance by preventing myocellular accumulation of ceramide

evidence.quote: We suggest that during fasting, women are relatively protected from FFA-induced insulin resistance by preventing myocellular accumulation of ceramide

narrative_results.statement: Women displayed a higher rate of lipolysis when expressed as flux per resting energy expenditure

evidence.quote: if expressed as flux per REE, women displayed a higher rate of lipolysis

discrepancies.pass_2_value: Pass 1 specifies [6,6-2H]Glucose while Pass 2 specifies [6,6-2H2]Glucose; these are different isotope labelings with different numbers of deuterium atoms
Pass_1 describes measurement parameters (VO2, VCO2) while pass_2 describes timing (final 20 minutes); these are different aspects and appear misaligned

structural_discrepancies.pass_1_value: infusions of insulin (60mU/m2·min) (Actrapid 100 IU/ml; NovoNordisk FarmaB.V., Alphen aan den Rijn, The Netherlands)
structural_discrepancies.pass_2_value: Thereafter (T = 3 h), infusions of insulin (60 mU/m2·min) (Actrapid 100 IU/ml; NovoNordisk FarmaB.V., Alphen aan den Rijn, The Netherlands) and glucose 20% (to maintain a plasma glucose level of 5 mmol/liter) were started.

structural_discrepancies.pass_1_value: Plasma glucose concentrations were measured with the glucose oxidase method using a Beckman Glucose Analyzer 2 (Beckman, Palo Alto, CA) with intraassay variation of 2–3%.
structural_discrepancies.pass_2_value: At T = 8h, five blood samples were drawn at 5-min intervals for determination of glucose and glycerol enrichments.
five blood samples drawn at 5-min intervals

structural_discrepancies.pass_1_value: Beckman Glucose Analyzer 2 using glucose oxidase method

Plasma FFA concentrations were determined with an enzymatic colorimetric method (NEFA-C test kit; Wako Chemicals GmbH, Neuss, Germany) with intraassay variation of 1%, interassay variation of 4–15%, and detection limit of 0.02 mmol/liter.
structural_discrepancies.pass_2_value: Plasma glucose levels were measured every 5 min at the bedside.

structural_discrepancies.pass_1_value: NEFA-C test kit enzymatic colorimetric method

Insulin and cortisol were determined on an Immulite 2000 system (Diagnostic Products Corp., Los Angeles, CA). Insulin was measured with a chemiluminescent immunometric assay with intraassay variation of 3–6%, interassay variation of 4–6%, and detection limit of 15 pmol/liter.
structural_discrepancies.pass_2_value: Body composition was measured with bioelectrical impedance analysis (Maltron BF906; Maltron, Rayleigh, UK).

structural_discrepancies.pass_1_value: Cortisol was measured with a chemiluminescent immunoassay with intraassay variation of 7–8%, interassay variation of 7–8%, and detection limit of 50 nmol/liter.
structural_discrepancies.pass_2_value: Oxygen consumption (VO2) and CO2 production (VCO2) were measured continuously during the final 20 min of both the basal state and the hyperinsulinemic euglycemic clamp by indirect calorimetry using a ventilated hood system (Sensormedics model 2900; Sensormedics, Anaheim, CA).
final 20 minutes of basal state and clamp

structural_discrepancies.pass_1_value: Glucagon was determined with the Linco 125I RIA (Linco Research, St. Charles, MO) with an intraassay variation of 9–10%, interassay variation of 5–7%, and detection limit of 15 ng/liter.
structural_discrepancies.pass_2_value: Muscle biopsies were performed to assess muscle content of ceramide, glucosylceramide, and FAT/CD36 at the end of both the basal state and the hyperinsulinemic euglycemic clamp.
Pro-Mag I biopsy needle under local anesthesia
end of basal state and end of hyperinsulinemic euglycemic clamp

structural_discrepancies.pass_1_value: Adiponectin was determined by a RIA (Linco) with intraassay variation of 2–7%, interassay variation of 16–17%, and detection limit of 1 ng/ml.
structural_discrepancies.pass_2_value: Plasma glucose concentrations were measured with the glucose oxidase method using a Beckman Glucose Analyzer 2 (Beckman, Palo Alto, CA) with intraassay variation of 2–3%.

structural_discrepancies.pass_1_value: Muscle biopsies were performed to assess muscle content of ceramide, glucosylceramide, and FAT/CD36 at the end of both the basal state and the hyperinsulinemic euglycemic clamp.
structural_discrepancies.pass_2_value: Plasma FFA concentrations were determined with an enzymatic colorimetric method (NEFA-C test kit; Wako Chemicals GmbH, Neuss, Germany) with intraassay variation of 1%, interassay variation of 4–15%, and detection limit of 0.02 mmol/liter.

End of basal state and end of hyperinsulinemic euglycemic clamp
Body composition was measured with bioelectrical impedance analysis (Maltron BF906; Maltron, Rayleigh, UK).
structural_discrepancies.pass_2_value: Insulin was measured with a chemiluminescent immunometric assay with intraassay variation of 3–6%, interassay variation of 4–6%, and detection limit of 15 pmol/liter.

structural_discrepancies.pass_1_value: Oxygen consumption (VO2) and CO2 production (VCO2) were measured continuously during the final 20 min of both the basal state and the hyperinsulinemic euglycemic clamp by indirect calorimetry using a ventilated hood system (Sensormedics model 2900; Sensormedics, Anaheim, CA).
structural_discrepancies.pass_2_value: Cortisol was measured with a chemiluminescent immunoassay with intraassay variation of 7–8%, interassay variation of 7–8%, and detection limit of 50 nmol/liter.

structural_discrepancies.pass_1_value: Ceramide and glucosylceramide in muscle biopsies were measured with an HPLC method as described (33).
structural_discrepancies.pass_2_value: Glucagon was determined with the Linco 125I RIA (Linco Research, St. Charles, MO) with an intraassay variation of 9–10%, interassay variation of 5–7%, and detection limit of 15 ng/liter.

structural_discrepancies.pass_1_value: At T = 8h, five blood samples were drawn at 5-min intervals for determination of glucose and glycerol enrichments.
structural_discrepancies.pass_2_value: Ceramide and glucosylceramide in muscle biopsies were measured with an HPLC method as described (33). Muscle biopsies were weighed and homogenized in 300 μl water by sonification, and 50 μl muscle homogenates was used.
muscle biopsies homogenized and analyzed by HPLC

semantic_groups.rationale: Both describe age inclusion criterion of 18-35 years; only difference is singular 'criterion' vs plural 'criteria'
Both describe BMI inclusion criterion of 20-25 kg/m²; differences are singular vs plural and formatting of superscript (² vs 2)
Identical extraction of fasting duration specification
Pass 1 specifies [6,6-2H]Glucose while Pass 2 specifies [6,6-2H2]Glucose; these are different isotope labelings with different numbers of deuterium atoms
Identical extraction of glycerol stable isotope tracer specification
Both specify 60 mU/m²·min insulin infusion; Pass 2 adds timing information (T=3h) not present in Pass 1, and formatting differs (² vs 2)
Both describe glucose measurement using glucose oxidase method on Beckman Glucose Analyzer 2; information presented in different order
Both describe plasma FFA measurement using enzymatic colorimetric method with NEFA-C test kit; information presented in different order
Both describe insulin measurement using Immulite 2000 chemiluminescent immunometric assay; minor wording differences (assay vs method) are negligible
Both describe cortisol measurement using Immulite 2000 chemiluminescent immunoassay; terminology differences (assay vs method) are negligible
Both describe glucagon measurement using Linco 125I RIA; pass_2 provides more complete instrument name while pass_1 provides assay type classification
Pass_1 contains adiponectin measurement information (Linco RIA) but pass_2 is empty; orphan entry requires human review
Both describe muscle biopsy timing and method identically; only capitalization difference (End vs end) is negligible
Both describe body composition measurement using Maltron BF906 bioelectrical impedance analysis; pass_1 is more complete but conveys same fact
Pass_1 describes measurement parameters (VO2, VCO2) while pass_2 describes timing (final 20 minutes); these are different aspects and appear misaligned
Both describe ceramide/glucosylceramide measurement using HPLC method; pass_2 provides additional context about sample preparation but same core fact
Both describe blood sampling at 5-minute intervals. Pass 2 adds context (T=8h, glucose enrichment) but the core fact about sampling frequency is identical.
Both state plasma glucose measured every 5 minutes at bedside. Pass 2 adds instrument detail (Beckman Glucose Analyzer 2) but core timing fact is the same.
Identical fact with only capitalization difference (Follicular vs follicular phase).
Both describe weight-maintaining diet with at least 250g carbohydrates for 3 days. Minor wording variation but same underlying requirement.
Identical participant count and gender distribution.
Pass 1 is empty/missing while Pass 2 contains FAT/CD36 measurement method information. This is an orphan entry from Pass 2.
Identical statements about basal plasma FFA levels in women vs men
Identical statements about insulin-mediated glucose uptake between sexes
Identical statements about muscle ceramide gender differences in basal state
Identical statements about ceramide-glucose uptake correlation
Identical statements about muscle fatty acid translocase/CD36 between sexes
Identical statements about female protection from FFA-induced insulin resistance via ceramide prevention
Identical statements about adiponectin levels in females across basal and clamp states
Identical statements about plasma epinephrine levels in females vs males during hyperinsulinemic euglycemic clamp
Pass 1 contains a finding about lipolysis rates in women; Pass 2 is empty
Both extractions report identical finding: plasma glucose levels significantly lower in women than men
Pass 2 contains a finding about basal endogenous glucose production in women; Pass 1 is empty
